1. LER .harness/progress.txt → histórico da sessão
2. LER .harness/features.json → próxima feature (passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENTAR uma feature por vez
4. TESTAR visualmente (bun tauri dev)
5. COMMIT após feature passar
6. ATUALIZAR .harness/progress.txt

Vou começar lendo os arquivos de estado do harness.

file.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly 6 renamed Text/TextContent to Content
- Page component uses masthead prop, not header
- Label colors: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orangered, grey, yellow

## F010: PASSED ✅
- Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 running
- Icons generated from SVG source

## Next steps:
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F009 Complete - State Management Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created src/state/store.ts with @zazcart/tools
- Defined 5 slices:
  - ui: activeTab, theme, projectOrder, sidebarCollapsed, logPanelHeight
  - projects: Record<string, Project>
  - processes: Record<string, Process>
  - logs: entries with ring buffer (max 10000), autoScroll, filter
  - features: items[], loading, error
- Defined TypeScript interfaces: Project, Feature, Process, LogEntry
- Created selectors: activeProject, projectsArray, activeProcesses, logsForProcess, featureStats
- Created src/state/index.ts for exports
- Updated App.tsx to display state info

## Key learnings:
- @zazcart/tools useMachine hook causes infinite loop with getSnapshot
- Added @ts-nocheck temporarily due to type inference issues
- Using local useState as fallback until hook issues resolved

## F009: PASSED ✅
- State machine configured with 5 slices
- Types and selectors ready
- Visual test passed

## Next steps:
- F002: Implement Kanban board view

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F001 Complete - Multi-project Tab Management
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created TabBar component (src/components/TabBar.tsx)
- Implemented tab functionality:
  - Add new project tabs via (+) button
  - Close tabs via (X) button (only visible when >1 tab)
  - Select tabs to switch between projects
  - Drag-and-drop prepared (console.log for now)
- Updated App.tsx to integrate TabBar with mock projects
- Fixed PatternFly button nesting issue (span with role="button" instead)
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly Tab is already a button, cannot nest Button inside
- Use span with role="button" for accessible close button inside tabs
- isOverflowHorizontal prop handles tab overflow automatically

## F001: PASSED ✅
- TabBar renders project tabs
- Add/Close/Select functionality works
- Console clean (no errors)

## Next steps:
- F003: Implement real-time log panel

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F002 Complete - Kanban Board View
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created KanbanBoard component (src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx)
  - 6 columns: BACKLOG, TODO, DOING, REVIEW, QA, DONE
  - Badge with feature count per column
  - Features sorted by priority within columns
  - Horizontal scroll for responsive layout
- Created KanbanCard component (src/components/KanbanCard.tsx)
  - Expandable card with description and steps
  - Labels for priority (critical/high/medium/low)
  - Labels for category (core/ui/setup)
  - Visual icons for step completion status
- Integrated KanbanBoard into App.tsx with mock features
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues (removed deprecated isFlat prop)
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly 6 Card does not have isFlat prop
- Feature status mapping: "done"/"completed"/"passed" → DONE column
- Features with passes: true always go to DONE column

## F002: PASSED ✅
- 6-column Kanban board rendering correctly
- Cards expand to show description and steps
- Console clean (no errors)

## Next steps:
- F003: Implement real-time log panel with ANSI colors

================================================================================

## Overview

PatternFly is Red Hat's open source design system. It consists of components, documentation, and code for building enterprise applications at scale.

## NPM Package

The latest version is 6.3.0, last published 3 months ago. You can start using @patternfly/react-log-viewer in your project by running `npm i @patternfly/react-log-viewer`.(no content)There are 10 other projects in the npm registry using @patternfly/react-log-viewer.

## Usage Example

To use the LogViewer component, you can import it from the package like this:
```javascript
import { LogViewer, LogViewerSearch, LogViewerContext } from '@patternfly/react-log-viewer';
import { Button, Checkbox, Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarItem } from '@patternfly/react-core';
```

## Features (from OpenShift integration)

The PatternFly LogViewer extension was integrated into OpenShift Pods logs and Nodes logs. It keeps all the features of the previous log window (wrap lines, full screen, etc.), and brings new features.(no content)The PF log viewer is virtualized, which means it's performant when the logs are large.

## Recent Updates (PatternFly 5 → 6)

In recent releases, they exposed LogViewerProps to allow for reuse and fixed issues with the full-screen React demo.(no content)The final minor release within PatternFly 5 is now live, and PatternFly 6 is next.

- **GitHub Repository**: https://github.com/patternfly/react-log-viewer

# LogViewer Usage Examples

Based on the GitHub repository documentation, here are the key components and features:

## Core Components

The page references two main exported components:
- **LogViewer** - The primary log display component
- **LogViewerSearch** - Integrated search functionality

## Import Statement

```javascript
import { useState, Fragment, useRef } from 'react';
import { LogViewer, LogViewerSearch, LogViewerContext } from '@patternfly/react-log-viewer';
import { Button, Checkbox, Toolbar, ToolbarContent, ToolbarItem } from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { data } from '@patternfly/react-log-viewer/patternfly-docs/content/extensions/react-log-viewer/examples/realTestData.js';
```

## Available Examples

The documentation outlines six implementation patterns:

1. **Basic** - Simple LogViewer implementation
2. **With search** - Incorporates LogViewerSearch component
3. **With complex toolbar** - Uses Toolbar, ToolbarContent, and ToolbarItem
4. **With header component** - Custom header integration
5. **With footer component** - Custom footer integration
6. **With ANSI color logs** - Support for colored terminal output

## Key Features

- Context-based state management via LogViewerContext
- React hooks integration (useState, useRef)
- PatternFly design system components for consistent styling
- Customizable toolbar controls with Button and Checkbox elements
- Search capabilities built into the component library

**Note:** The actual code implementations are referenced but not fully displayed in the source document.
